**Analyzing Data Inputs**

I've been mapping out the application's feature set in detail. Specifically, I am planning the dashboard's design and data aggregation features. Visualization options are top of mind. The JSON state saving mechanism is under planning as well.

I'm now zeroing in on report generation. I'm focusing on a print-friendly view that doesn't rely on a backend. I'm leaning toward using CSS print media queries or a dedicated "Report Mode" component to render the content in a way that's easy to print or save as a PDF directly from the browser.


**Defining Aggregation Logic**

I'm now prioritizing aggregation logic over text analysis within the report. A placeholder for Gemini will handle the smart text generation later. I'm focusing on the `xlsx` parsing. Initially I can't `npm install`, so I'm investigating CDN options for the `xlsx` library, or a standard library approach. Since I'm limited in this environment, I'll simulate the logic or write a robust CSV/Excel parser. I'm considering using `<input type="file" />` and CSV parsing, or utilizing a CDN for an ESM-compatible `xlsx` or `read-excel-file` library. For safety, I will assume the `xlsx` is available.


**Finalizing Report Strategy**

I'm now prioritizing the report's structure. I've decided to create a dedicated "Report View" route. Users will simply print this view, which is the most reliable way to create the report. I am looking at the data aggregation now; the primary method will be to group the 15-minute data into hourly averages. I've decided I'll add the `<script>` tag for `xlsx` in `index.html`. My next step is to prepare the metadata definitions.


**Outlining Core Features**

I'm solidifying the report features. The system will handle data loading, processing, and display for reporting. This includes resampling 15-minute data to hourly averages and calculating stats. I'm focusing on the component breakdown, including a header, file upload, dashboard, and the printable report view. I'm also preparing for the state management system.


**Adding Gemini Analysis**

I've integrated Gemini's capabilities. I'm focusing on text analysis of the generated statistics, providing insights like peak consumption periods. I'm using the `GoogleGenAI` to create a textual summary. I'm also ensuring the application has strict data typing to maintain consistency. I have completed adding the `xlsx` CDN. Also, I have been using `recharts` for the dashboard visualization and focusing on a professional corporate color scheme for the styling. Finally, I've added a menu to the app with the company name, version, and my website.


**Adjusting File Handling**

I'm now refining the file upload process. Instead of 12 separate uploads, the system will offer a single upload field to select all files simultaneously. This should significantly enhance the user experience by simplifying the upload procedure. I am also working on stricter TypeScript and ensuring all imports are at the top level for better code organization. I'm also ensuring the Gemini API integration for text generation is in place.


**Refining Data Processing**

I'm now prioritizing the data aggregation logic. I've decided to add the `xlsx` script to `index.html` as a global to keep TypeScript happy. I'm building out the code to load, parse, and aggregate the data.  I'm using the `YYYY-MM-DD HH` format for grouping and averaging hourly values. For telemetry, an hourly average is a sensible approach. I'm focusing on defining core file structures and data handling.


**Determining Report Metrics**

I'm currently finalizing the report's key metrics. I've decided to include "Hourly Max" which is the peak 15-minute value, and "Hourly Min" representing the base load, for each hour. I'm focusing on defining and calculating these metrics directly within my data aggregation process to ensure efficient report generation.
